rationale: In the citing paper, Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) appears as a single example in a grouped list of prior studies that examine choices under internal, built-in constraints (e.g., hedonic vs utilitarian goods). The citing authors do not rely on this paper's specific findings, mechanisms, or methods to build their hypotheses or interpret their results. Because it functions only as broad background positioning with no distinct role for this specific source, the citation is best classified as a passing mention.
